# Verification Before Completion

## Overview

Claiming work is complete without verification is dishonesty, not efficiency.

**Core principle:** Evidence before claims, always.

**Violating the letter of this rule is violating the spirit of this rule.**

## The Iron Law

```
NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE
```

If you haven't run the verification command in this message, you cannot claim it passes.

## The Gate Function

```
BEFORE claiming any status or expressing satisfaction:

1. IDENTIFY: What command proves this claim?
2. RUN: Execute the FULL command (fresh, complete)
3. READ: Full output, check exit code, count failures
4. VERIFY: Does output confirm the claim?
   - If NO: State actual status with evidence
   - If YES: State claim WITH evidence
5. ONLY THEN: Make the claim

Skip any step = lying, not verifying
```

## Common Failures

| Claim | Requires | Not Sufficient |
|-------|----------|----------------|
| Tests pass | Test command output: 0 failures | Previous run, "should pass" |
| Linter clean | Linter output: 0 errors | Partial check, extrapolation |
| Build succeeds | Build command: exit 0 | Linter passing, logs look good |
| Bug fixed | Test original symptom: passes | Code changed, assumed fixed |
| Regression test works | Red-green cycle verified | Test passes once |
| Agent completed | VCS diff shows changes | Agent reports "success" |
| Requirements met | Line-by-line checklist | Tests passing |

## Red Flags - STOP

- Using "should", "probably", "seems to"
- Expressing satisfaction before verification ("Great!", "Perfect!", "Done!", etc.)
- About to commit/push/PR without verification
- Trusting agent success reports
- Relying on partial verification
- Thinking "just this once"
- Tired and wanting work over
- **ANY wording implying success without having run verification**

## Rationalization Prevention

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Should work now" | RUN the verification |
| "I'm confident" | Confidence ≠ evidence |
| "Just this once" | No exceptions |
| "Linter passed" | Linter ≠ compiler |
| "Agent said success" | Verify independently |
| "I'm tired" | Exhaustion ≠ excuse |
| "Partial check is enough" | Partial proves nothing |
| "Different words so rule doesn't apply" | Spirit over letter |

## Key Patterns

**Tests:**
```
✅ [Run test command] [See: 34/34 pass] "All tests pass"
❌ "Should pass now" / "Looks correct"
```

**Regression tests (TDD Red-Green):**
```
✅ Write → Run (pass) → Revert fix → Run (MUST FAIL) → Restore → Run (pass)
❌ "I've written a regression test" (without red-green verification)
```

**Build:**
```
✅ [Run build] [See: exit 0] "Build passes"
❌ "Linter passed" (linter doesn't check compilation)
```

**Requirements:**
```
✅ Re-read plan → Create checklist → Verify each → Report gaps or completion
❌ "Tests pass, phase complete"
```

**Agent delegation:**
```
✅ Agent reports success → Check VCS diff → Verify changes → Report actual state
❌ Trust agent report
```

## Why This Matters

From 24 failure memories:
- your human partner said "I don't believe you" - trust broken
- Undefined functions shipped - would crash
- Missing requirements shipped - incomplete features
- Time wasted on false completion → redirect → rework
- Violates: "Honesty is a core value. If you lie, you'll be replaced."

## When To Apply

**ALWAYS before:**
- ANY variation of success/completion claims
- ANY expression of satisfaction
- ANY positive statement about work state
- Committing, PR creation, task completion
- Moving to next task
- Delegating to agents

**Rule applies to:**
- Exact phrases
- Paraphrases and synonyms
- Implications of success
- ANY communication suggesting completion/correctness

## The Bottom Line

**No shortcuts for verification.**

Run the command. Read the output. THEN claim the result.

This is non-negotiable.

```text
1	import type { SpotAllRound, SpotAllTile } from './types';
2	
3	export type SpotAllPhase = 'playing' | 'round-complete' | 'game-over';
4	
5	export interface SpotAllState {
6	  rounds: SpotAllRound[];
7	  roundIndex: number;
8	  tiles: SpotAllTile[];
9	  selectedIds: Set<string>;
10	  wrongCooldownIds: Set<string>;
11	  phase: SpotAllPhase;
12	  retryCount: number;
13	}
14	
15	export type SpotAllAction =
16	  | { type: 'INIT_ROUNDS'; rounds: SpotAllRound[] }
17	  | { type: 'TAP_TILE'; tileId: string }
18	  | { type: 'CLEAR_WRONG_COOLDOWN'; tileId: string }
19	  | { type: 'ADVANCE_ROUND' }
20	  | { type: 'COMPLETE_GAME' };
21	
22	export const WRONG_COOLDOWN_MS = 600;
23	export const ROUND_ADVANCE_MS = 750;
24	
25	export const createInitialSpotAllState = (
26	  rounds: SpotAllRound[],
27	): SpotAllState => ({
28	  rounds,
29	  roundIndex: 0,
30	  tiles: rounds[0]?.tiles ?? [],
31	  selectedIds: new Set<string>(),
32	  wrongCooldownIds: new Set<string>(),
33	  phase: 'playing',
34	  retryCount: 0,
35	});
36	
37	export const spotAllReducer = (
38	  state: SpotAllState,
39	  action: SpotAllAction,
40	): SpotAllState => {
41	  switch (action.type) {
42	    case 'INIT_ROUNDS': {
43	      return createInitialSpotAllState(action.rounds);
44	    }
45	    case 'TAP_TILE': {
46	      if (state.phase !== 'playing') return state;
47	      if (state.wrongCooldownIds.has(action.tileId)) return state;
48	
49	      const tile = state.tiles.find((t) => t.id === action.tileId);
50	      if (!tile) return state;
51	
52	      if (tile.isCorrect && state.selectedIds.has(action.tileId)) {
53	        const next = new Set(state.selectedIds);
54	        next.delete(action.tileId);
55	        return { ...state, selectedIds: next };
56	      }
57	
58	      if (tile.isCorrect) {
59	        const next = new Set(state.selectedIds);
60	        next.add(action.tileId);
61	        const allCorrect = state.tiles
62	          .filter((t) => t.isCorrect)
63	          .every((t) => next.has(t.id));
64	        return {
65	          ...state,
66	          selectedIds: next,
67	          phase: allCorrect ? 'round-complete' : 'playing',
68	        };
69	      }
70	
71	      const cooldown = new Set(state.wrongCooldownIds);
72	      cooldown.add(action.tileId);
73	      return {
74	        ...state,
75	        wrongCooldownIds: cooldown,
76	        retryCount: state.retryCount + 1,
77	      };
78	    }
79	    case 'CLEAR_WRONG_COOLDOWN': {
80	      if (!state.wrongCooldownIds.has(action.tileId)) return state;
81	      const next = new Set(state.wrongCooldownIds);
82	      next.delete(action.tileId);
83	      return { ...state, wrongCooldownIds: next };
84	    }
85	    case 'ADVANCE_ROUND': {
86	      if (state.phase !== 'round-complete') return state;
87	      const nextIndex = state.roundIndex + 1;
88	      const nextRound = state.rounds[nextIndex];
89	      if (!nextRound) {
90	        return { ...state, phase: 'game-over' };
91	      }
92	      return {
93	        ...state,
94	        roundIndex: nextIndex,
95	        tiles: nextRound.tiles,
96	        selectedIds: new Set<string>(),
97	        wrongCooldownIds: new Set<string>(),
98	        phase: 'playing',
99	      };
100	    }
101	    case 'COMPLETE_GAME': {
102	      return { ...state, phase: 'game-over' };
103	    }
104	    default: {
105	      return state;
106	    }
107	  }
108	};
109
```

 `./worktrees/feat/confusable-spotall/src/games/spot-all/build-spot-all-round.ts`

```text
1	import { nanoid } from 'nanoid';
2	import { pickVariation } from './visual-variation/pick-variation';
3	import type { SpotAllConfig, SpotAllRound, SpotAllTile } from './types';
4	import type { DistractorSourceContext } from '@/lib/distractors/types';
5	import { composeDistractors } from '@/lib/distractors/compose';
6	import { listSources } from '@/lib/distractors/registry';
7	
8	export interface BuildSpotAllRoundOptions {
9	  rng?: () => number;
10	  forceTarget?: string;
11	}
12	
13	const pickTarget = (
14	  config: SpotAllConfig,
15	  rng: () => number,
16	  forceTarget?: string,
17	): string => {
18	  if (forceTarget) return forceTarget;
19	
20	  const pairChars = config.selectedConfusablePairs.flatMap(
21	    (p) => p.pair,
22	  );
23	  const reversibleChars = config.selectedReversibleChars;
24	  const pool = [...new Set([...pairChars, ...reversibleChars])];
25	  if (pool.length === 0) return 'b';
26	  return pool[Math.floor(rng() * pool.length)]!;
27	};
28	
29	export const buildSpotAllRound = (
30	  config: SpotAllConfig,
31	  options: BuildSpotAllRoundOptions = {},
32	): SpotAllRound => {
33	  const rng = options.rng ?? Math.random;
34	  const target = pickTarget(config, rng, options.forceTarget);
35	
36	  const ctx: DistractorSourceContext = {
37	    selectedConfusablePairs: config.selectedConfusablePairs,
38	    selectedReversibleChars: config.selectedReversibleChars,
39	  };
40	
41	  const candidates = composeDistractors(
42	    listSources(),
43	    target,
44	    ctx,
45	    config.distractorCount,
46	    rng,
47	  );
48	
49	  const distractorTiles: SpotAllTile[] = candidates.map((c) => ({
50	    id: nanoid(),
51	    label: c.label,
52	    isCorrect: false,
53	    transform: c.transform,
54	    visualVariation: config.visualVariationEnabled
55	      ? pickVariation(rng, config.enabledFontIds)
56	      : undefined,
57	  }));
58	
59	  const safeCorrectCount = Math.max(1, config.correctTileCount);
60	
61	  const correctTiles: SpotAllTile[] = Array.from(
62	    { length: safeCorrectCount },
63	    () => ({
64	      id: nanoid(),
65	      label: target,
66	      isCorrect: true,
67	      visualVariation: config.visualVariationEnabled
68	        ? pickVariation(rng, config.enabledFontIds)
69	        : undefined,
70	    }),
71	  );
72	
73	  const tiles = shuffle([...correctTiles, ...distractorTiles], rng);
74	  return { target, tiles, correctCount: correctTiles.length };
75	};
76	
77	const shuffle = <T>(items: T[], rng: () => number): T[] => {
78	  const out = [...items];
79	  for (let i = out.length - 1; i > 0; i--) {
80	    const j = Math.floor(rng() * (i + 1));
81	    [out[i], out[j]] = [out[j]!, out[i]!];
82	  }
83	  return out;
84	};
85
```

```text
53	      config.roundsInOrder,
54	      seed,
55	    );
56	    return order.map((index) => ordered[index]!).filter(Boolean);
57	  }, [config, seed, sessionEpoch]);
58	
59	  const [state, dispatch] = useReducer(
60	    spotAllReducer,
61	    rounds,
62	    createInitialSpotAllState,
63	  );
64	
65	  useEffect(() => {
66	    dispatch({ type: 'INIT_ROUNDS', rounds });
67	    const reset = globalThis.setTimeout(() => {
68	      setScoreVisible(false);
69	      setGameOverVisible(false);
70	    }, 0);
71	    return () => globalThis.clearTimeout(reset);
72	  }, [rounds]);
73	
74	  useEffect(() => {
75	    const prev = phaseRef.current;
76	    phaseRef.current = state.phase;
77	
78	    if (state.phase === 'round-complete' && prev !== 'round-complete') {
79	      const show = globalThis.setTimeout(() => {
80	        setScoreVisible(true);
81	        playSound('round-complete');
82	      }, 0);
83	      const hide = globalThis.setTimeout(() => {
84	        setScoreVisible(false);
85	        dispatch({ type: 'ADVANCE_ROUND' });
86	      }, ROUND_ADVANCE_MS);
87	      return () => {
88	        globalThis.clearTimeout(show);
89	        globalThis.clearTimeout(hide);
90	      };
91	    }
92	
93	    if (state.phase === 'game-over' && prev !== 'game-over') {
94	      const timer = globalThis.setTimeout(() => {
95	        setGameOverVisible(true);
96	        playSound('game-complete');
97	      }, 400);
98	      return () => globalThis.clearTimeout(timer);
99	    }
100	  }, [state.phase]);
101	
102	  useEffect(() => {
103	    if (state.wrongCooldownIds.size === 0) return;
104	    const timers = [...state.wrongCooldownIds].map((tileId) =>
105	      globalThis.setTimeout(() => {
106	        dispatch({ type: 'CLEAR_WRONG_COOLDOWN', tileId });
107	      }, WRONG_COOLDOWN_MS),
108	    );
109	    return () => {
110	      for (const timer of timers) globalThis.clearTimeout(timer);
111	    };
112	  }, [state.wrongCooldownIds]);
113	
114	  const handleTap = (tileId: string): void => {
115	    const tile = state.tiles.find((tl) => tl.id === tileId);
116	    if (!tile) return;
117	    if (state.wrongCooldownIds.has(tileId)) return;
118	    if (state.phase !== 'playing') return;
119	    if (tile.isCorrect && !state.selectedIds.has(tileId)) {
120	      playSound('correct');
121	    } else if (!tile.isCorrect) {
122	      playSound('wrong');
123	    }
124	    dispatch({ type: 'TAP_TILE', tileId });
125	  };
126	
127	  const handlePlayAgain = (): void => setSessionEpoch((e) => e + 1);
128	  const handleHome = (): void => {
129	    void navigate({ to: '/$locale', params: { locale } });
130	  };
131	
132	  const round = rounds[state.roundIndex];
133	
134	  if (!round) return <></>;
135	
136	  return (
137	    <div
138	      className={`game-container skin-${skin.id} flex w-full flex-col items-center gap-6`}
139	      style={skin.tokens as CSSProperties}
140	    >
141	      {skin.SceneBackground ? <skin.SceneBackground /> : null}
142	      <ProgressHUD
143	        roundIndex={state.roundIndex}
144	        totalRounds={rounds.length}
145	        levelIndex={0}
146	        isLevelMode={false}
147	        phase={
148	          state.phase === 'round-complete'
149	            ? 'round-complete'
150	            : 'playing'
151	        }
152	        showDots
153	        showFraction
154	        showLevel={false}
155	      />
156	      <SpotAllPrompt
157	        target={round.target}
158	        ttsEnabled={config.ttsEnabled}
159	      />
160	      <SpotAllGrid state={state} onTap={handleTap} />
161	      {skin.RoundCompleteEffect ? (
162	        <skin.RoundCompleteEffect visible={scoreVisible} />
163	      ) : (
164	        <ScoreAnimation visible={scoreVisible} />
165	      )}
166	      {gameOverVisible &&
167	        (skin.CelebrationOverlay ? (
168	          <skin.CelebrationOverlay
169	            retryCount={state.retryCount}
170	            onPlayAgain={handlePlayAgain}
171	            onHome={handleHome}
172	          />
173	        ) : (
174	          <GameOverOverlay
175	            retryCount={state.retryCount}
176	            onPlayAgain={handlePlayAgain}
177	            onHome={handleHome}
178	          />
179	        ))}
180	      <span className="sr-only" aria-live="polite">
181	        {t('spot-all-ui.prompt', { target: round.target })}
182	      </span>
183	    </div>
184	  );
185	};
```

```text
1	import type {
2	  DistractorCandidate,
3	  DistractorSource,
4	  DistractorSourceContext,
5	} from './types';
6	
7	const shuffle = <T>(items: T[], rng?: () => number): T[] => {
8	  const out = [...items];
9	  const random = rng ?? (() => Math.random());
10	  for (let i = out.length - 1; i > 0; i--) {
11	    const j = Math.floor(random() * (i + 1));
12	    [out[i], out[j]] = [out[j]!, out[i]!];
13	  }
14	  return out;
15	};
16	
17	export const composeDistractors = (
18	  sources: DistractorSource[],
19	  target: string,
20	  ctx: DistractorSourceContext,
21	  count: number,
22	  rng?: () => number,
23	): DistractorCandidate[] => {
24	  const pool: DistractorCandidate[] = [];
25	  for (const s of sources) pool.push(...s.getCandidates(target, ctx));
26	
27	  const seen = new Set<string>();
28	  const unique = pool.filter((c) => {
29	    const key = `${c.label}|${c.transform ?? ''}`;
30	    if (seen.has(key)) return false;
31	    seen.add(key);
32	    return true;
33	  });
34	
35	  return shuffle(unique, rng).slice(0, count);
36	};
37
```

```text
140	/**
141	 * Deterministic default round values for NumberMatch. Using a fixed sequence
142	 * (rather than `Math.random`) keeps VR baselines stable across runs — nanoid
143	 * seeds in the route loader bypass any Math.random pin, so the only reliable
144	 * fix is to remove randomness from the default config entirely. Users still
145	 * customise via the settings panel.
146	 */
147	const DEFAULT_NUMBER_MATCH_ROUND_VALUES: readonly number[] = [5, 8, 3];
148	
149	const makeDefaultNumberMatchConfig = (): NumberMatchConfig => ({
150	  gameId: 'number-match',
151	  component: 'NumberMatch',
152	  inputMethod: 'drag',
153	  wrongTileBehavior: 'lock-auto-eject',
154	  tileBankMode: 'distractors',
155	  distractorCount: 2,
156	  totalRounds: 3,
157	  // Deterministic order by default — see DEFAULT_PICTURE_CONFIG comment.
158	  roundsInOrder: true,
159	  ttsEnabled: true,
160	  mode: 'numeral-to-group',
161	  tileStyle: 'dots',
162	  range: { min: 1, max: 12 },
163	  rounds: DEFAULT_NUMBER_MATCH_ROUND_VALUES.map((value) => ({ value })),
164	});
165	
166	const makeDefaultSpotAllConfig = (): SpotAllConfig =>
167	  resolveSpotAllSimpleConfig({
168	    configMode: 'simple',
169	    selectedConfusablePairs: [
170	      { pair: ['b', 'd'], type: 'mirror-horizontal' },
171	      { pair: ['p', 'q'], type: 'mirror-horizontal' },
172	    ],
173	    selectedReversibleChars: [],
174	  });
175	
176	export const resizeNumberMatchRounds = (
177	  prev: NumberMatchRound[],
178	  count: number,
179	  range: { min: number; max: number },
180	): NumberMatchRound[] => {
181	  const n = Math.max(1, Math.min(count, 50));
182	  const span = Math.max(1, range.max - range.min + 1);
183	  // Deterministic fill: cycle through the range starting at `range.min`. This
184	  // avoids `Math.random` so defaults stay stable (see note above) and is
185	  // simple enough for users to understand at a glance. They can override any
186	  // round value via the settings panel.
187	  const fillValueAt = (index: number): number =>
188	    range.min + (index % span);
189	
190	  // Detect a collapsed state: every round has the same value even though the
191	  // range allows variety. This happens when the user narrows the range down
192	  // to a single value (collapsing every round to that value) and then widens
193	  // it again — without this check the preserved values stay stuck at the
194	  // collapse point and the player sees the same number every round. Also
195	  // handles the empty-prev case from a legacy saved config.
196	  const firstValue = prev[0]?.value;
197	  const allCollapsed =
198	    prev.length >= 2 &&
199	    span > 1 &&
200	    prev.every((r) => r.value === firstValue);
201	  if (prev.length === 0 || allCollapsed) {
202	    return Array.from({ length: n }, (_, i) => ({
203	      value: fillValueAt(i),
204	    }));
205	  }
206	
207	  const next = prev.slice(0, n).map((r, i) => {
208	    if (r.value >= range.min && r.value <= range.max) return { ...r };
209	    return { value: fillValueAt(i) };
210	  });
211	  while (next.length < n) {
212	    next.push({ value: fillValueAt(next.length) });
213	  }
214	  return next;
215	};

223	    quantity: 5,

240	      : undefined,
241	    source: base.source

243	      : undefined,
244	  };
245
```

```text
3	export const confusablePairsSource: DistractorSource = {
4	  id: 'confusable-pairs',
5	  getCandidates(target, { selectedConfusablePairs = [] }) {
6	    const out: DistractorCandidate[] = [];
7	    for (const sel of selectedConfusablePairs) {
8	      const [left, right] = sel.pair;
9	      if (left === target) {
10	        out.push({
11	          label: right,
12	          sourceId: 'confusable-pairs',
13	          meta: { relationshipType: sel.type },
14	        });
15	      } else if (right === target) {
16	        out.push({
17	          label: left,
18	          sourceId: 'confusable-pairs',
19	          meta: { relationshipType: sel.type },
20	        });
21	      }
22	    }
23	    return out;
24	  },
25	};
```

```bash
fix(spot-all): flex-wrap grid layout and border-based focus ring

- SpotAllGrid: swap responsive grid-cols for flex-wrap so tiles flow
  naturally at any count instead of forcing fixed column counts
- SpotAllTile: replace outline-based focus ring with focus-visible:border-primary
  (simpler, avoids the outline-style/inline-box-shadow bugs from prior
  two fix commits); move transparent border to class so inline style
  only carries state overrides; narrow transition to bg/border/transform

Made-with: Claude
```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-01 02:11:44Z)**_

UI fixes committed. Now here's my full analysis:

---

## PR Review

The implementation is solid — the spec, plan, and commits are well-aligned. All 12 original gaps are addressed (R5b, GameShell/skin, ProgressHUD, per-tap validate, distractor library, Storybook, VR, E2E, i18n, grouped picker, font pool, tile appearance). The just-committed flex-wrap + border-focus fixes are good.

---

## Your 4 Questions

### 1. Instructions panel shown on every refresh — is this a bug?

**Yes, confirmed bug.** SpotAll's `showInstructions` is hardcoded to `useState(true)` at `$gameId.tsx:775`. Every other game initialises it with `useState(draftState === null)` — meaning they skip instructions when resuming a session.

SpotAll also **has no game state persistence at all** — there is no `draftState` wiring, no session history index write, and no resume flow. Rounds are rebuilt fresh on every mount. So even if you fixed the `showInstructions` init, mid-game progress would be lost on refresh.

Two separate issues:
- **Quick fix**: pass `draftState` to `SpotAllGameBody` and use `useState(draftState === null)` for `showInstructions` (same as other games). But SpotAll's body function doesn't even accept a `draftState` prop yet.
- **Full fix (follow-up)**: wire SpotAll into the session-history-index machinery the way WordSpell is, so in-progress rounds persist across refresh. This is a bigger effort — it was filed as part of the follow-up issues (#257).

### 2. Distractor count bug (8 correct + 8 distractor = only 9 tiles shown)

**Yes, confirmed bug.** Here's the root cause:

`compose.ts:35` does `shuffle(unique, rng).slice(0, count)`. The pool is only as large as what the sources produce. `confusable-pairs.ts` only emits **one candidate per selected pair** that contains the target. With the default config (`b↔d`, `p↔q`), if the target is `b`, the pool has exactly **1 distractor**: `d`. Even if you set `distractorCount: 8`, you get 1.

The code silently truncates — there's no padding, no warning. Asking for 8 distractors but getting 1 is the expected wrong behaviour given the current pool logic.

**To get 16 tiles total (8 correct + 8 distractor)**, you'd need to either:
- Select many more confusable pairs/groups (so the pool has ≥8 unique candidates for the target), OR
- Implement a padding strategy: repeat allowed distractors to fill up to `distractorCount`

This is a real gap in the spec — it only says "compose distractors from sources", but doesn't define what to do when the pool is smaller than `distractorCount`. Worth filing an issue or noting in the plan.

### 3. Rebase with simplified + advanced form PR?

**Yes, you need to resync.** Run `/resync` to pull the latest master and rebase this branch.

### 4. Remove the "Visual Variation" top-level toggle?

**Yes, agreed — it should be removed.** The cleaner model is: **Font Pool empty = no variation**, **Font Pool non-empty = variation active**. This is already how it would intuitively work. The separate master toggle is redundant and confusing.

However, your second point is the important design question: **variation currently applies to both correct tiles AND distractor tiles** (see `buildSpotAllRound.ts:54` and `:67` — both call `pickVariation`). The current toggle controls both.

If the intent is:
- Variation on all tiles: just remove the toggle, derive from `enabledFontIds.length > 0`
- Variation only on distractors (to make them harder to spot): separate font pool per tile type, needs replanning
- Variation only on correct tiles (unusual): also needs replanning

I'd recommend deciding this before removing the toggle. If "both tiles vary" is the right design, just remove the toggle and replace with `enabledFontIds.length > 0` — that's a small contained change. If you want per-tile-type variation control, that's a new spec delta.

---

SpotAll therefore ships its own\nreducer and tile component as a workaround.\n\nTo unify the games, we need to:\n\n1. **Generalise `Slot`** with a `mode: 'drag' | 'tap-select'` prop. Every round is built with exactly those numbers.\n\n## Desired behaviour\n\nAllow teachers to configure a **range** (min–max) for both values so each\nround's tile layout varies:\n\n- `correctTileCount: { min: 2, max: 4 }` → each round picks a random count\n  in that window\n- `distractorCount: { min: 3, max: 8 }` → same\n\nAlternatively expose a single **randomise** toggle per field that samples\nfrom the full valid range.\n\n## Acceptance criteria\n\n- [ ] `SpotAllConfig` updated — `correctTileCount` and `distractorCount`\n  accept `number | { min: number; max: number }` (backwards-compatible:\n  bare number = fixed, object = range)\n- [ ] `buildSpotAllRound` reads the range and samples with the seeded RNG\n- [ ] `SpotAllConfigForm` exposes range inputs (dual-handle slider or two\n  number inputs labelled Min / Max) for both fields in the simple form\n- [ ] `resolveSimpleConfig` defaults remain fixed numbers (no behaviour\n  change for existing saved configs)\n- [ ] Unit tests for the range-sampling path in `buildSpotAllRound`\n\n## Related\n\nPart of the SpotAll redesign (#252).","state":"OPEN","title":"feat(spot-all): range-based correct-tile and distractor counts"}

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Create bug issue for instructions + state persistence

```bash
gh issue create \
  --title "bug(spot-all): instructions overlay shown on every page refresh; no mid-game state persistence" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Symptoms

1. **Instructions always shown on refresh** — \`SpotAllGameBody\` initialises
   \`showInstructions\` with \`useState(true)\` (hardcoded) at
   \`src/routes/\$locale/_app/game/\$gameId.tsx:775\`.
   All other game bodies use \`useState(draftState === null)\`, which skips
   instructions when a session is being resumed.

2. **No mid-game state persistence** — \`SpotAllGameBody\` does not receive or
   thread a \`draftState\` prop. The component also does not write session state to
   the session-history-index, so refreshing mid-game starts a completely fresh game
   with a new seed.

## Root cause

\`SpotAllGameBody\` was added to the route without connecting it to the
session-persistence layer that \`WordSpellGameBody\` and others rely on.
The route loader (\`$gameId.tsx:1262\`) does set \`phase: 'instructions'\` on
new \`AnswerGameDraftState\` objects, but SpotAll never reads from or writes to
that state.

## Fix

### Quick (instructions skip only)

Pass \`draftState\` into \`SpotAllGameBody\` from the route component and replace:

\`\`\`ts
const [showInstructions, setShowInstructions] = useState(true);
\`\`\`

with:

\`\`\`ts
const [showInstructions, setShowInstructions] = useState(draftState === null);
\`\`\`

This at minimum prevents the instructions flash on a hard refresh when the
browser already has a session record.

### Full (round-state persistence)

Wire SpotAll into the session-history-index write path so that the current
\`roundIndex\`, \`selectedIds\`, and \`phase\` survive a refresh. This requires:

- Serialising \`SpotAllState\` into the draftState schema
- Writing on each \`ADVANCE_ROUND\` / \`TAP_TILE\` dispatch
- Rehydrating on mount when \`draftState !== null\`

This work overlaps with #257 (the \`useGameRound\` extraction), which would
centralise the lifecycle and make persistence easier to wire uniformly.

## Part of

SpotAll redesign (#252 / #257).
EOF
)" \
  --label "bug"
```


```bash
## Silent distractor truncation (found in review of #252)

There is an existing bug that this issue's implementation must address:

**Problem:** \`composeDistractors\` in \`src/lib/distractors/compose.ts\` does
\`shuffle(unique).slice(0, count)\`. The pool is bounded by what the selected
confusable pairs produce — for a typical 2-pair config (b↔d, p↔q), the
target character has exactly **one** confusable character in the pool.
Asking for \`distractorCount: 8\` silently returns 1 tile.

**Observed:** 8 correct + 8 distractors configured → 9 tiles rendered (8 correct, 1 distractor).

**Required behaviour for this issue:** When implementing range-based counts,
\`buildSpotAllRound\` must also handle the case where \`pool.length < requested count\`.
Options:
- Cap distractors to \`pool.length\` and surface a warning to the teacher
- Pad by repeating pool candidates with distinct IDs (same character, different tile)
- Require a minimum pool size and validate in \`resolveSimpleConfig\`

Padding by repetition is the least surprising behaviour for teachers who want
"fill the screen" density — add this to the acceptance criteria.
EOF
)"
```

```text
+const resolveSpotAllConfig = (
+  saved: Record<string, unknown> | null,
+): SpotAllConfig => {
+  const fallback = makeDefaultSpotAllConfig();
+  if (!saved || typeof saved !== 'object') return fallback;
+
+  const merged = { ...fallback, ...saved } as SpotAllConfig;
+  if (
+    (!Array.isArray(merged.selectedConfusablePairs) ||
+      merged.selectedConfusablePairs.length === 0) &&
+    (!Array.isArray(merged.selectedReversibleChars) ||
+      merged.selectedReversibleChars.length === 0)
+  ) {
+    return fallback;
+  }
+  return merged;
+};
```

```text
const SpotAllGameBody = ({
  gameId,
  sessionId,
  seed,
  gameSpecificConfig,
  customGameId,
  customGameName,
  customGameColor,
  customGameCover,
  debug,
}: {
  gameId: string;
  sessionId: string;
  seed: string;
  gameSpecificConfig: Record<string, unknown> | null;
  customGameId: string | null;
  customGameName: string | null;
  customGameColor: string | null;
  customGameCover: Cover | null;
  debug: boolean;
}): JSX.Element => {
  const { t } = useTranslation('games');
  const { save, update, remove, customGames } = useCustomGames();
  const { isBookmarked, toggle } = useBookmarks();
  const bookmarkTarget = customGameId
    ? ({ targetType: 'customGame', targetId: customGameId } as const)
    : ({ targetType: 'game', targetId: gameId } as const);
  const navigate = useNavigate({ from: '/$locale/game/$gameId' });
  const existingCustomGameNames = useMemo(
    () =>
      customGames.filter((d) => d.gameId === gameId).map((d) => d.name),
    [customGames, gameId],
  );
  const initial = useMemo(
    () => resolveSpotAllConfig(gameSpecificConfig),
    [gameSpecificConfig],
  );
  const [cfg, setCfg] = useState(initial);
  const [showInstructions, setShowInstructions] = useState(true);
  useEffect(() => {
    setCfg(initial);
  }, [initial]);
  usePersistLastGameConfig(
    gameId,
    cfg as unknown as Record<string, unknown>,
  );

  const debugPanel = debug ? (
    <DebugPanel
      gameId={gameId}
      resolvedConfig={cfg as unknown as Record<string, unknown>}
      rawSavedConfig={gameSpecificConfig}
      customGame={{
        id: customGameId,
        name: customGameName,
        color: customGameColor,
        cover: customGameCover,
      }}
      session={{
        sessionId,
        seed,
        draftState: null,
        persistedContent: null,
      }}
      rounds={[]}
    />
  ) : null;

  if (showInstructions) {
    return (
      <>
        <InstructionsOverlay
          text={t('instructions.spot-all')}
          onStart={() => setShowInstructions(false)}
          ttsEnabled={cfg.ttsEnabled}
          gameTitle={t('spot-all')}
          gameId={gameId}
          cover={customGameCover ?? undefined}
          customGameId={customGameId ?? undefined}
          customGameName={customGameName ?? undefined}
          customGameColor={
            (customGameColor ?? undefined) as GameColorKey | undefined
          }
          config={cfg as unknown as Record<string, unknown>}
          onConfigChange={(config) =>
            setCfg(resolveSpotAllConfig(config))
          }
          onSaveCustomGame={async ({ name, color, config, cover }) =>
            save({
              gameId,
              name,
              color,
              config,
              cover,
            })
          }
          onUpdateCustomGame={
            customGameId
              ? async (name, config, extras) => {
                  await update(customGameId, config, name, extras);
                }
              : undefined
          }
          onDeleteCustomGame={
            customGameId
              ? async (id) => {
                  await remove(id);
                  await navigate({
                    search: (prev) => ({
                      ...prev,
                      configId: undefined,
                    }),
                  });
                }
              : undefined
          }
          existingCustomGameNames={existingCustomGameNames}
          isBookmarked={isBookmarked(bookmarkTarget)}
          onToggleBookmark={() => void toggle(bookmarkTarget)}
        />
        {debugPanel}
      </>
    );
  }
```

```text
  onDeleteCustomGame?: (configId: string) => Promise<void>;
  /** Persist current draft as the "last session" snapshot for this gameId. */

    config: Record<string, unknown>,
  ) => Promise<void> | void;
  existingCustomGameNames?: readonly string[];
  isBookmarked?: boolean;
  onToggleBookmark?: () => void;
};

export const InstructionsOverlay = ({
  text,
  onStart,
  ttsEnabled,
  gameTitle,
  gameId,
  cover,
  customGameId,
  customGameName,
  customGameColor = DEFAULT_GAME_COLOR,

  onSaveCustomGame,
```

45/45 pass. Now let me implement the "tile styles" rename — remove `visualVariationEnabled` toggle, rename the type, and derive variation from font pool.

Before diving in, my naming suggestion: **`TileStyle`** / **`tileStyles`** for the concept. In the config form UI: **"Tile Styles"** as the section header, with "Font" and future "Color" as sub-pools. In code: `SpotAllTileStyle` replaces `SpotAllVisualVariation`, `enabledTileStyleIds` stays as two pools (`enabledFontIds`, future `enabledColorIds`), and variation is derived from `enabledFontIds.length > 0`.

```text
1	import { FONT_POOL, SIZE_POOL } from './pools';
2	
3	export interface SpotAllVisualVariation {
4	  fontFamily?: string;
5	  fontSizePx: number;
6	}
7	
8	export const pickVariation = (
9	  rng: () => number,
10	  enabledFontIds: readonly string[],
11	): SpotAllVisualVariation => {
12	  const fonts = FONT_POOL.filter((f) => enabledFontIds.includes(f.id));
13	  const font =
14	    fonts.length > 0
15	      ? fonts[Math.floor(rng() * fonts.length)]
16	      : undefined;
17	  return {
18	    fontFamily: font?.family,
19	    fontSizePx: SIZE_POOL[Math.floor(rng() * SIZE_POOL.length)]!,
20	  };
21	};
22
```

```text
127	  const visualVariationEnabled =

131	  ];
132	
133	  const isFontEnabled = (fontId: string): boolean =>
134	    enabledFontIds.includes(fontId);
135	  const allFontsOn = FONT_POOL.every((f) => isFontEnabled(f.id));
136	
137	  const toggleVisualVariation = (): void => {
138	    onChange({
139	      ...current,
140	      visualVariationEnabled: !visualVariationEnabled,
141	    });
142	  };
143	
144	  const toggleAllFonts = (): void => {

148	
149	  const toggleFont = (fontId: string): void => {

155

172	                  : 'bg-muted text-foreground',
173	            ].join(' ')}
174	          >
175	            {t(`spot-all-ui.config.groups.${group.id}`)}
176	          </button>
177	          <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2 pl-2">
178	            {group.chips.map((chip) => (
179	              <button
180	                key={chipKey(chip)}
181	                type="button"
182	                onClick={() => toggleChip(chip)}
183	                className={[
184	                  'rounded-full border px-3 py-1 text-sm',
185	                  isChipSelected(chip)
186	                    ? 'border-primary bg-primary/20'
187	                    : 'border-border bg-card',
188	                ].join(' ')}
189	              >
190	                <ChipLabel chip={chip} />
191	              </button>
192	            ))}
193	          </div>
194	        </div>
195	      ))}
196	      <div className="flex flex-col gap-2">
197	        <button
198	          type="button"

200	          className={[

202	            visualVariationEnabled

238	                </button>
```

**Issues filed:**

- Pulled in 20 master commits, including the unified config state PR (#254)
- Resolved 6 binary VR baseline conflicts (took master's versions — need Docker re-run after this PR merges)
- Fixed the only TypeScript break: `usePersistLastGameConfig` was removed in the master refactor; `SpotAllGameBody` still called it

**Route fix (also fixes part of #278):**
- Added `draftState` prop to `SpotAllGameBody`
- `showInstructions` now uses `useState(draftState === null)` — same as WordSpell/NumberMatch/SortNumbers
- Replaced `usePersistLastGameConfig` with `persistLastSessionConfig` from `useCustomGames()` + `onPersistLastSession` on `InstructionsOverlay`

**Tile styles (Q4):**
- `SpotAllVisualVariation` → `SpotAllTileStyle`
- `tile.visualVariation` → `tile.tileStyle` everywhere
- `visualVariationEnabled` removed from config, types, configFields, resolve-simple-config, all tests and stories
- Variation now activates automatically when `enabledFontIds.length > 0` — empty font pool = no styles
- Config form: two-level hierarchy ("Visual Variation" toggle + "Font Pool" sub-header) collapsed into a single flat **"Tile styles"** header that acts as all-on/all-off for the font chips; partially-enabled state shown with `bg-primary/40`
- i18n updated (en + pt-BR)

45/45 tests pass, typecheck clean, all pre-push hooks green.

```text
30	    if (!ttsEnabled || !prompt) return;
31	    let cancelled = false;
32	    void whenSoundEnds().then(() => {
33	      if (!cancelled) speakPrompt();
34	    });
35	    return () => {
36	      cancelled = true;
37	    };
38	    // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps -- re-speak only when target/ttsEnabled changes, not on every speakPrompt re-creation
39	  }, [target, ttsEnabled]);
40	
41	  return (
42	    <div className="flex items-center justify-center gap-3">
43	      <p className="text-center text-2xl font-semibold text-foreground">
44	        {prompt}
45	      </p>
46	      {ttsEnabled && (
47	        <button
48	          type="button"
49	          aria-label={t('spot-all-ui.speak-prompt')}
50	          className="flex size-10 shrink-0 items-center justify-center rounded-full shadow-md active:scale-95"
51	          style={{
52	            background: 'var(--skin-question-audio-bg)',
53	            color: 'var(--skin-question-audio-fg)',
54	          }}
55	          onClick={speakPrompt}
56	        >
57	          <Volume2 size={20} aria-hidden="true" />
58	        </button>
59	      )}
60	    </div>
61	  );
62	};
63
```

```text
function plainSettingsFromQuery(
  doc: SettingsDoc | null | { toJSON: () => SettingsDoc },
): SettingsDoc | null {
  if (doc === null) {

  if (
    typeof (doc as { toJSON?: () => SettingsDoc }).toJSON === 'function'
  ) {
    return (doc as { toJSON: () => SettingsDoc }).toJSON();

  return doc as SettingsDoc;
}
```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-01 03:22:50Z)**_

Found it. `SpotAll.stories.tsx` uses `decorators: [withDb, withRouter]` — `withDb` provides the database needed by `useSettings()`. `SpotAllPrompt.stories.tsx` has no decorators at all, so `useSettings()` throws.

Quick fix:

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-01 03:24:09Z)**_

Pushed. The fix was a single missing decorator: `SpotAllPrompt.stories.tsx` calls `useSettings()` which needs the RxDB database provider, but unlike `SpotAll.stories.tsx` it had no `decorators: [withDb]`. Added that and the smoke-test will pass now.
